payload.message:
## STEP 0: Beads task tracking (MANDATORY — do this FIRST before anything else) Run these exact commands: ``` (cd /root && BEADS_ACTOR=openclaw bd create --title="NR Document Watcher $(date +%Y-%m-%d)" --description="Poll NextRequest for new public records docs, analyze, send Telegram follow-up" --type=task --priority=3) > /tmp/bd_issue.txt 2>/dev/null ISSUE=$(grep "Created issue:" /tmp/bd_issue.txt | grep -oE "[a-zA-Z]+-[a-z0-9]+" | head -n 1 || echo "") echo "BEADS_ISSUE=$ISSUE" [ -n "$ISSUE" ] && (cd /root && BEADS_ACTOR=openclaw bd update "$ISSUE" --claim) 2>/dev/null && echo "Claimed $ISSUE" ``` Save the ISSUE value (e.g. HOME-abc). You will close it at the end. --- Run the NR document watcher, then analyze any new docs and send a follow-up Telegram message. STEP 1 — Run the orchestrator: python3 /opt/records-monitor/nr-orchestrator.py 2>&1 The orchestrator polls NextRequest for new public records documents, downloads them, uploads to Drive, sends a notification Telegram, and writes /tmp/nr-new-docs.json with the results. STEP 2 — Check /tmp/nr-new-docs.json: cat /tmp/nr-new-docs.json If new_docs array is empty: Close beads issue: (cd /root && BEADS_ACTOR=openclaw bd close "$ISSUE" --reason="NR watcher: no new docs") 2>/dev/null Then stop here (NO_REPLY). STEP 3 — For each unique PRR that has new docs: a) Extract text from each PDF: pdftotext "" - 2>/dev/null | head -400 (use local_path from the JSON; if pdftotext fails, note the filename only) b) Read the "subject" field — this is what was originally requested c) Analyze: - What time period does this release cover? - What doc types were provided (reports, emails, contracts, invoices)? - What is in each doc vs. what the subject says was requested? - Are there obvious gaps? (time gaps, missing doc types, redacted pages, boilerplate non-responses) d) If gaps exist: draft a deficiency/follow-up letter STEP 4 — Send the analysis via Telegram (one message per PRR): curl -s -X POST "https://api.telegram.org/botYOUR_TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN/sendMessage" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d "{\"chat_id\":\"YOUR_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID\",\"text\":\"\",\"parse_mode\":\"HTML\"}" Format the message as HTML: 🔍 Analysis: PRR {pid} ({dept}) Requested: {subject} Received: {N} doc(s) — {date} Content summary: {1-2 lines per doc: title/filename, date range if present, key topics} Gap analysis: {specific gaps: missing years, doc types not produced, redacted sections} If no gaps: ✅ Release appears complete Recommended action: {send deficiency letter | no action needed} If deficiency letter recommended, append: 
Via NextRequest Portal — PRR {pid}

Dear Records Custodian,

I am writing regarding my CPRA request #{pid} submitted to {dept}. The request sought: {subject}.

On {date}, you released {N} document(s). The release is incomplete because:

{numbered list of specific deficiencies}

Under Gov. Code § 7920 et seq., please produce the missing records within 14 days, or provide written exemption citations for any withheld records.

Barklees Sanders
Keep the full message under 4000 characters. If too long, truncate at 3800 chars. ## FINAL STEP: Close beads issue After sending all messages (or if stopping early), run: ``` (cd /root && BEADS_ACTOR=openclaw bd close "$ISSUE" --reason="NR watcher done: new docs analyzed") 2>/dev/null ```
